title: Unpatched Software and Outdated Systems
description: Learn how unpatched software and outdated systems expose organizations to known vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit to gain access to sensitive data.

attack_stages.title: Outdated System
attack_stages.description: The organization is running software that has not been updated with security patches.

attack_stages.title: Vulnerability Discovery
attack_stages.description: Attackers identify known security flaws in outdated software.

attack_stages.title: Exploit Execution
attack_stages.description: Attackers exploit known vulnerabilities to run malicious code.

attack_stages.title: Backdoor Access
attack_stages.description: Attackers create hidden access points for persistent entry.

attack_stages.title: Data Compromise
attack_stages.description: Sensitive data is accessed, stolen, or modified.

prevention_points.title: Regular Software Updates
prevention_points.description: Apply security patches as soon as they are released by vendors.

prevention_points.title: Asset Monitoring
prevention_points.description: Maintain an inventory of all systems and track software versions.

prevention_points.title: Security Hardening
prevention_points.description: Disable unnecessary services and reduce attack surface.

prevention_points.title: Intrusion Detection
prevention_points.description: Monitor systems for unauthorized persistence mechanisms.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A company operates a database system that relies on several software components, some of which have not been updated in a long time.

nodes.title: What is Unpatched Software?
nodes.text: Unpatched software refers to systems or applications that have known security vulnerabilities that have not been fixed through updates or patches. These vulnerabilities are publicly known and often documented.

nodes.title: Security Weakness
nodes.text: Outdated systems and end-of-life software no longer receive security updates, making them especially vulnerable. Attackers actively search for these systems because their weaknesses are already known.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to exploit known vulnerabilities in outdated software to gain unauthorized access to the database system.

choices.text: Known vulnerabilities can be exploited by attackers
They automatically become more secure over time

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Unpatched systems remain vulnerable because known flaws are not fixed. Attackers actively search for these weaknesses.

nodes.title: Exploit Execution
nodes.text: The attacker successfully exploits a known vulnerability in the outdated software, allowing them to execute malicious code on the system.

nodes.title: Backdoor Access
nodes.text: The attacker creates a hidden access method (backdoor) to maintain persistent access even after system restarts or partial fixes.

nodes.title: Data Compromise
nodes.text: Sensitive data stored in the database can now be accessed, modified, or stolen due to the exploited vulnerability.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should regularly update and patch software, monitor system versions, replace end-of-life systems, and maintain a strict upgrade policy aligned with security and compliance requirements.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how an outdated system allowed a known vulnerability to be exploited, leading to full compromise of the database environment.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Unpatched software is one of the most common causes of security breaches. Regular updates and system modernization are critical to prevent attackers from exploiting known vulnerabilities.
